name: PAN Card
issuingAuthority: Income Tax Department, Government of India (through Protean eGov / UTI Infrastructure)
dataSource: incometax.gov.in and incometaxindia.gov.in — official government websites

sections.purpose: PAN (Permanent Account Number) is a 10-character alphanumeric unique identifier issued by the Income Tax Department of India. It is mandatory for: (1) Filing Income Tax Returns. (2) Financial transactions above prescribed limits (bank deposits, FDs, mutual funds, etc.). (3) Opening bank accounts. (4) Purchasing or selling property above ₹10 lakh. (5) Tax Deducted at Source (TDS). (6) Foreign exchange transactions. (7) Widely accepted as photo identity proof. (8) Linking with Aadhaar for government services. e-PAN (PDF with QR code) is the primary format; physical card is optional. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in
sections.eligibility: Any Indian citizen (individual, HUF, company, firm, LLP, trust, etc.) is eligible. No age limit — minors can have PAN. NRIs and foreign nationals with income in India are eligible. One PAN per person/entity — holding multiple PANs is illegal and punishable with a penalty of ₹10,000 under Section 272B of Income Tax Act. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in
documents: Form 49A: Official application form for Indian citizens — available at incometax.gov.in or Protean portal
Proof of Identity (any one): Aadhaar card, Voter ID (EPIC), Passport, Driving Licence, Ration Card with photo, Government-issued photo ID
Proof of Address (any one): Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Passport, Driving Licence, Bank account statement, Electricity/Telephone/Gas bill (not older than 3 months), Rent agreement
Proof of Date of Birth (any one): Birth Certificate, SSLC/Matriculation Certificate, Passport, Driving Licence, Aadhaar card with DOB
Two recent passport-size colour photographs (3.5cm x 2.5cm, white background) — if applying physically or at a facilitation centre. Source: tin.tin.proteantech.in
Aadhaar card (for Instant e-PAN — must have Aadhaar linked mobile for OTP)
sections.fees: Physical PAN card (Indian address dispatch): ₹91 excluding GST (approximately ₹107 inclusive of GST). Source: incometaxindia.gov.in. Physical PAN card (foreign address dispatch): ₹862 excluding GST. Instant e-PAN (via Aadhaar OTP at incometax.gov.in): completely FREE. Source: incometax.gov.in. Changes/corrections to existing PAN data: same fee as fresh application (₹107 approx). Note: fee is subject to GST and may change — verify at the official portal at time of application.
sections.processingTime: Instant e-PAN: generated in minutes (requires Aadhaar with linked mobile). Regular online application (Protean/UTI): PAN number generated in 7–15 working days. e-PAN (PDF): sent to registered email within 24 hours of PAN number generation. Physical PAN card: delivered via post in 15–20 days after PAN number generation. Source: incometax.gov.in
sections.validity: PAN card has no expiry date and is valid for lifetime. However, PAN must be linked with Aadhaar by the deadline set by the Income Tax Department — unlinked PANs may become inoperative. Source: incometax.gov.in
applicationSteps: Step 1 — Visit incometax.gov.in → Quick Links → Instant e-PAN
Step 2 — Enter your valid Aadhaar number
Step 3 — Confirm Aadhaar details and OTP verification on registered mobile
Step 4 — e-PAN generated immediately as downloadable PDF — free of cost
Option B — Regular Application (Physical card + e-PAN):
Step 1 — Visit tin.tin.proteantech.in or utiitsl.com → Apply for New PAN
Step 2 — Fill Form 49A with all required details
Step 3 — Upload proof of identity, address, DOB, and photograph
Step 4 — Pay fee online (approximately ₹107 inclusive of GST for Indian address)
Step 5 — Submit application and note the 15-digit Acknowledgement Number
Step 6 — PAN number generated in 7–15 working days; e-PAN sent to email
Step 7 — Physical PAN card delivered in 15–20 days via post
sections.panAadhaarLinking: PAN must be linked with Aadhaar as per Income Tax Department mandate. Link at incometax.gov.in → e-Filing → Link Aadhaar. Enter PAN and Aadhaar → OTP verification. Late fee applicable if linking done after deadline. Unlinked PAN becomes inoperative and cannot be used for financial transactions, ITR filing, or refunds. Source: incometax.gov.in
sections.lostPan: If PAN card is lost: Your PAN number never changes. Apply for reprint/duplicate at tin.tin.proteantech.in or utiitsl.com → Changes/Corrections → provide your existing PAN number. Fee: approximately ₹107. Download e-PAN free at incometax.gov.in. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in
sections.correction: To correct name, DOB, address, or other details: Apply for 'Changes or Correction in existing PAN data' at tin.tin.proteantech.in or utiitsl.com. Submit existing PAN number and supporting document showing correct information. Fee: approximately ₹107. New card issued in 15–20 days. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in
sections.tracking: Track PAN application status: Visit tin.tin.proteantech.in → Track PAN Status → Enter Acknowledgement Number. Or call Protean helpline: 020-27218080. Source: Protean (formerly NSDL eGov)
commonMistakes: Applying for a second PAN — illegal, punishable with ₹10,000 penalty under Section 272B of Income Tax Act. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in
Name mismatch with Aadhaar — causes PAN-Aadhaar linking failure
Not linking PAN with Aadhaar before deadline — PAN becomes inoperative
Using unofficial websites claiming to process PAN applications
Wrong date of birth — causes issues with ITR filing and TDS credit

edgeCases.title: Have two PAN numbers — what to do?
edgeCases.problem: Applicant accidentally has two PAN numbers. This is illegal under Section 272B of Income Tax Act.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Surrender the duplicate PAN immediately. Apply for surrender at tin.tin.proteantech.in → Changes/Corrections → mention both PAN numbers and request cancellation of the duplicate. Or write to your Income Tax Assessing Officer. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

edgeCases.title: PAN inoperative due to non-linking with Aadhaar
edgeCases.problem: PAN became inoperative because it was not linked with Aadhaar by the Income Tax Department deadline.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Link PAN with Aadhaar immediately at incometax.gov.in → Link Aadhaar. Pay applicable late fee. PAN reactivated after successful linking. Source: incometax.gov.in

faqs.question: What is PAN card?
faqs.answer: PAN (Permanent Account Number) is a 10-character alphanumeric identifier issued by the Income Tax Department. Mandatory for tax filing, financial transactions, bank accounts, and accepted as photo identity proof. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

faqs.question: How to get instant free PAN card?
faqs.answer: Visit incometax.gov.in → Quick Links → Instant e-PAN. Enter Aadhaar number → OTP on Aadhaar-linked mobile → e-PAN generated in minutes as free PDF. Source: incometax.gov.in

faqs.question: What is the fee for PAN card?
faqs.answer: Physical card (Indian address): approx ₹107 inclusive of GST. Instant e-PAN via Aadhaar: FREE. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

faqs.question: What documents are required for PAN?
faqs.answer: Form 49A, Proof of Identity, Proof of Address, Proof of DOB (Aadhaar serves for all three), two photographs (physical application). Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

faqs.question: How to link PAN with Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Link at incometax.gov.in → e-Filing → Link Aadhaar. Enter PAN and Aadhaar → OTP verification. Late fee may apply after deadline. Unlinked PAN becomes inoperative. Source: incometax.gov.in

faqs.question: What if I lost my PAN card?
faqs.answer: PAN number never changes. Download e-PAN free at incometax.gov.in. Apply for physical reprint at tin.tin.proteantech.in (fee: approx ₹107). Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

faqs.question: How to correct name on PAN?
faqs.answer: Apply for 'Changes or Correction' at tin.tin.proteantech.in. Submit existing PAN + document with correct name. Fee: approx ₹107. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

faqs.question: Can I have two PAN numbers?
faqs.answer: No. Holding multiple PANs is illegal under Section 272B of Income Tax Act with ₹10,000 penalty. Surrender duplicate immediately at tin.tin.proteantech.in or to your Assessing Officer. Source: incometaxindia.gov.in

faqs.question: How long does PAN card take?
faqs.answer: Instant e-PAN: minutes. Regular: 7–15 working days for PAN number; 15–20 days for physical card. Source: incometax.gov.in

faqs.question: How to track PAN application?
faqs.answer: Track at tin.tin.proteantech.in → Track PAN Status → Enter Acknowledgement Number. Or call 020-27218080. Source: Protean eGov (formerly NSDL)
